title: Publications
text:
Here is the complete list of our research publications:


Auger, Patrick, Thomas Lavigne, Benjamin Smaniotto, Mario Spagnuolo, Francesco dell’Isola, and François Hild. 2020. “Poynting Effects in Pantographic Metamaterial Captured via Multiscale DVC.” The Journal of Strain Analysis for Engineering Design 56 (7): 462–77. https://doi.org/10.1177/0309324720976625.


Lavigne, T., S. P. A. Bordas, and J. Lengiewicz. 2023. “Identification of Material Parameters and Traction Field for Soft Bodies in Contact.” Computer Methods in Applied Mechanics and Engineering 406 (March): 115889. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cma.2023.115889.


Lavigne, Thomas, Camilo Andrés Suarez Afanador, Anas Obeidat, and Stéphane Urcun. 2025. Synthetic Porous Microstructures: Automatic Design, Simulation, and Permeability Analysis. https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.14518.


Lavigne, Thomas, Stéphane Urcun, Bérengère Fromy, et al. 2025. “Hierarchical Poromechanical Approach to Investigate the Impact of Mechanical Loading on Human Skin Micro-Circulation.” International Journal for Numerical Methods in Biomedical Engineering, ahead of print. https://doi.org/10.1002/cnm.70066.


Lavigne, Thomas, Stéphane Urcun, Emmanuelle Jacquet, et al. 2024. “Poromechanical Modelling of the Time-Dependent Response of in Vivo Human Skin During Extension.” In International Journal for Numerical Methods in Biomedical Engineering. International Journal for Numerical Methods in Biomedical Engineering. https://doi.org/10.48550/ARXIV.2412.07374.


Lavigne, Thomas, Stéphane Urcun, Pierre-Yves Rohan, Giuseppe Sciumè, Davide Baroli, and Stéphane P. A. Bordas. 2023. “Single and Bi-Compartment Poro-Elastic Model of Perfused Biological Soft Tissues: FEniCSx Implementation and Tutorial.” Journal of the Mechanical Behavior of Biomedical Materials 143 (July): 105902. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jmbbm.2023.105902.


Lavigne, T., A. Mazier, A. Perney, S. P. A. Bordas, F. Hild, and J. Lengiewicz. 2022. “Digital Volume Correlation for Large Deformations of Soft Tissues: Pipeline and Proof of Concept for the Application to Breast Ex Vivo Deformations.” Journal of the Mechanical Behavior of Biomedical Materials 136 (December): 105490. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jmbbm.2022.105490.


Lavigne, T., G. Sciumè, S. Laporte, et al. 2022. “Société de Biomécanique Young Investigator Award 2021: Numerical Investigation of the Time-Dependent Stress–Strain Mechanical Behaviour of Skeletal Muscle Tissue in the Context of Pressure Ulcer Prevention.” Clinical Biomechanics 93 (March): 105592. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.clinbiomech.2022.105592.

title: Research Activities
text:
A adapter comme projects de ma page
Our team specializes in the continuum mechanics of soft, living, and active materials. By focusing on constitutive modeling, visco-hyperelasticity, and fluid-structure interactions, we tackle critical challenges in physiology and tissue engineering.
Jump to: [ Cornea Mechanics href="#cornea" ] | [ Ocular Mechanics href="#eye" ] | [ Blood Clots & Hemostasis href="#clot" ] | [ Hydrogels & Biomaterials href="#hydrogel" ] | [ Other Tissues href="#others" ]

title: Research Activities
text:
## Corneal Mechanics & Refractive Surgery We investigate the intricate lamellar microstructure of the cornea to predict its response to surgical interventions (like LASIK or cross-linking) and degenerative diseases (such as keratoconus). Our approach combines inflation tests with microstructure-informed finite element models to characterize anisotropy and collagen remodeling. * Key topics: Corneal anisotropy, refractive surgery simulation, collagen fiber remodeling models. * [ Relevant Publications href="#/publications?tag=cornea" ]

 ## Full Ocular Mechanics Beyond the cornea, we model the mechanical environment of the entire eye globe. This includes analyzing the biomechanical factors contributing to glaucoma (optic nerve head deformation) and vitreoretinal interaction, treating the eye as a complex, pressurized multi-material system. * Key topics: Glaucoma biomechanics, optic nerve head deformation, intraocular pressure modeling. * [ Relevant Publications href="#/publications?tag=eye" ]

 ## Mechanics of Blood Clots & Hemostasis Understanding the mechanics of thrombus formation and dissolution is crucial for tackling cardiovascular diseases. We characterize the viscoelastic and fracture properties of fibrin networks and blood clots under physiological flow conditions, aiming to predict clot stability, embolization risks, and the efficacy of thrombolytic therapies. * Key topics: Thrombus visco-hyperelasticity, fracture mechanics of fibrin, fluid-structure interaction in thrombosis. * [ Relevant Publications href="#/publications?tag=clot" ]

 ## Synthetic Hydrogels & Biomimetic Materials Hydrogels are essential tools for tissue engineering and mechanobiology. We study the nonlinear, time-dependent mechanics of synthetic and natural hydrogels to tailor their properties for specific biomedical applications, such as developing scaffolds that mimic the mechanical niche of Smooth Muscle Cells (SMCs). * Key topics: Viscoelasticity of hydrogels, porous media mechanics, biomimetic scaffold design. * [ Relevant Publications href="#/publications?tag=hydrogel" ]

 ## Fundamental Soft Matter Mechanics & Others We also apply our expertise to general problems in soft matter and active tissue mechanics, including cell-matrix interactions and the fundamental behavior of other soft biological tissues (vascular, skin, etc.) using novel experimental and numerical tools. * Key topics: Smooth muscle cell mechanics, tissue growth and atrophy, inverse modeling techniques. * [ Relevant Publications href="#/publications?tag=others" ]

ANR KAYO (Leading group) : Microrheology of venous blood clots for the diagnosis of hypercoagulability
